info.name: Knowledge-Assist — Slack & opportunities
info.description:
Local/dev API calls for Slack OAuth helpers, opportunity + slack source setup, and sync trigger.

**Important:** `POST /opportunities/slack` does **not** take a Slack channel name. Pass `opportunity_id` (opportunity id), `name`, and `owner_email`. Channel names in Slack must *start with* the prefix derived from `opportunity_id` (see runbook).

query.description: URL-encoded full callback URL; must match Slack app Redirect URLs.
Must match users.email; sent as OAuth state.

request.description:
**When:** First time connecting Slack (or after revoke). Open `auth_url` in a browser — not usually used as a simple POST/GET automation without browser.

**Response:** `{"auth_url": "https://slack.com/oauth/v2/authorize?..."}`

**When:** New opportunity (`opportunity_id`) that should sync from Slack, OR existing opp missing `opportunity_sources` slack row.

**Not when:** New Slack channel only, same `opportunity_id` — no DB call; just `POST /sync/trigger`.

**Body fields:** `opportunity_id` = canonical opportunity id (e.g. `oid1023`). `name` = display name. `owner_email` = user who has `slack_access_token`.

Slack channel names must **start with** the alphanumeric prefix derived from `opportunity_id` (e.g. `oid1023` → channels like `oid1023-general`).

**When:** After OAuth + DB rows exist; and any time you want to pull latest Slack messages to GCS.

**Response:** `status: completed`, `items_total`, and `results[]` with per-`opportunity_id` `items_synced` and `ok`. Request waits until sync finishes.

**When:** You want to onboard opportunities from **existing** Slack channel names without calling `POST /opportunities/slack` per deal.

**Body:** none.

**Requires:** A user with `users.slack_access_token` (Slack OAuth). Optional env: `SLACK_CONNECTOR_USER_EMAIL`.

**Response:** `channels_total`, `channels_matched`, `opportunities_created`, `opportunity_sources_created`, `skipped` (channels that did not match OID/prefix rules).

item.name: Gmail — Discover threads by subject (DB upsert)

request.description:
**When:** Onboard `opportunities` + `gmail` source rows from threads whose **Subject** contains an opportunity id token (e.g. `oid1234`).

**Body:** none.

**Requires:** `users.google_refresh_token` (Google OAuth with gmail.readonly). Env: `GMAIL_DISCOVER_QUERY` (default subject search), optional `GMAIL_CONNECTOR_USER_EMAIL` or `DRIVE_CONNECTOR_USER_EMAIL`, `GMAIL_DISCOVER_MAX_THREADS`.

**Response:** `threads_scanned`, `threads_with_oid`, `opportunities_created`, `opportunity_sources_created`, `skipped_thread_ids`.

item.name: Drive — Discover folders under Requirements (DB upsert)

request.description:
**When:** Before `/sync/trigger` if you want to automatically onboard **new** Drive folders created under `DRIVE_ROOT_FOLDER_NAME` (e.g. `Requirements/`).

**Body:** none.

**Requires:**
- `DRIVE_ROOT_FOLDER_NAME={{driveRootFolderName}}` set in env (configs)
- A connector user has completed Google OAuth (users.google_refresh_token)

**Response:** summary of discovered subfolders and DB rows created.

item.name: Sync — Run (Slack + Gmail + Drive discover + sync)

request.description:
**When:** Scheduler or one-shot full pipeline: Slack discover → Gmail discover → Drive discover → run all plugins (`opportunity_sources`).

**Body:** none.

**Response:** `slack_discover`, `gmail_discover`, `drive_discover`, and `sync` (same shape as `/sync/trigger`).
